name: getFlagShow
description: Returns whether deep-sky object rendering is currently enabled. When disabled, no nebulae, galaxies, or star clusters will be displayed.
returnDescription: true if DSO rendering is enabled, false if disabled

name: setFlagShow
description: Enables or disables rendering of all deep-sky objects (nebulae, galaxies, star clusters, etc.).

parameters.name: enabled
parameters.description: true to show deep-sky objects, false to hide them

name: getFlagHints
description: Returns whether nebula hints (symbol markers for DSOs) are currently visible.
returnDescription: true if hints are visible, false if hidden

name: setFlagHints
description: Shows or hides nebula hints (symbol markers for DSOs). Hints help locate deep-sky objects on the sky.

parameters.name: visible
parameters.description: true to show hints, false to hide

name: getHintsAmount
description: Returns the amount of nebula hints displayed. Higher values show fainter objects. The actual amount is also affected by the current field of view.
example:
var hintsAmount = NebulaMgr.getHintsAmount();
core.debug("Hints amount: " + hintsAmount);

name: setHintsAmount
description: Sets the amount of nebula hints displayed. 0 = no hints, 10 = maximum hints (shows faintest DSOs).

name: getLabelsAmount
description: Returns the amount of nebula labels displayed. Higher values show labels for fainter objects.
example:
var labelsAmount = NebulaMgr.getLabelsAmount();
core.debug("Labels amount: " + labelsAmount);

name: setLabelsAmount
description: Sets the amount of nebula labels displayed. 0 = no labels, 10 = maximum labels (shows labels for faintest DSOs).

name: getHintsBrightness
description: Returns the brightness of nebula hint markers (0.0 to 1.0).
example:
var brightness = NebulaMgr.getHintsBrightness();
core.debug("Hints brightness: " + brightness);

name: setHintsBrightness
description: Sets the brightness of nebula hint markers. 0 = invisible, 1 = full brightness.

name: getLabelsBrightness
description: Returns the brightness of nebula labels (0.0 to 1.0).
example:
var labelBrightness = NebulaMgr.getLabelsBrightness();
core.debug("Labels brightness: " + labelBrightness);

name: setLabelsBrightness
description: Sets the brightness of nebula labels. 0 = invisible, 1 = full brightness.

name: getLabelsColor
description: Returns the current color of deep-sky object labels. IMPORTANT: For reading color values, use core.getStelProperty('NebulaMgr.labelsColor') instead of direct access.

name: setLabelsColor
description: Sets the color of deep-sky object labels.

parameters.name: color
parameters.description: RGB color vector (R, G, B) with values between 0.0 and 1.0

name: setHintsFadeDuration
description: Sets the fade duration for nebula hints when they appear or disappear. Controls how smoothly hints transition between visible and hidden states.

parameters.name: duration
parameters.description: Fade duration in seconds (0 = instant, 2 = slow fade)

name: getHintsProportional
description: Returns whether hints (symbols) are scaled proportionally to the actual angular size of the deep-sky object. When enabled, larger objects have larger symbols.

name: setHintsProportional
description: Sets whether hints (symbols) should be scaled proportionally to the actual angular size of the deep-sky object.

parameters.name: proportional
parameters.description: true to scale hints with object size, false to use fixed size

name: getFlagOutlines
description: Returns whether outlines are displayed for large deep-sky objects instead of simple hint symbols.

name: setFlagOutlines
description: Enables or disables outlines for large deep-sky objects. Outlines show the actual shape of large DSOs like the Veil Nebula or Andromeda Galaxy.

parameters.name: enabled
parameters.description: true to show outlines, false to use standard hints

name: getFlagAdditionalNames
description: Returns whether additional names (aliases) for deep-sky objects are displayed in the information panel.

name: setFlagAdditionalNames
description: Enables or disables display of additional names (aliases) for deep-sky objects in the information panel.

parameters.name: enabled
parameters.description: true to show additional names, false to hide

name: getDesignationUsage
description: Returns whether catalog designations (e.g., 'M31', 'NGC 224') are used instead of common names for labels.

name: setDesignationUsage
description: Sets whether catalog designations (like 'M31') should be used instead of common names for object labels.

parameters.name: enabled
parameters.description: true to use catalog designations, false to use common names

name: getFlagSurfaceBrightnessUsage
description: Returns whether surface brightness is used instead of integrated magnitude for determining DSO visibility and hint display.

name: setFlagSurfaceBrightnessUsage
description: Sets whether surface brightness should be used instead of integrated magnitude for visibility calculations. More accurate for extended objects like galaxies.

parameters.name: enabled
parameters.description: true to use surface brightness, false to use integrated magnitude

name: getFlagSurfaceBrightnessArcsecUsage
description: Returns whether surface brightness is displayed in mag/arcsec² (instead of mag/arcmin²).

name: setFlagSurfaceBrightnessArcsecUsage
description: Sets the unit for surface brightness display. mag/arcsec² is the professional astronomical standard.

parameters.name: enabled
parameters.description: true for mag/arcsec², false for mag/arcmin²

name: getFlagSurfaceBrightnessShortNotationUsage
description: Returns whether short notation (m/□') is used for surface brightness instead of full text.

name: setFlagSurfaceBrightnessShortNotationUsage
description: Sets whether short notation (e.g., 'm/□') should be used for surface brightness display.

parameters.name: enabled
parameters.description: true for short notation, false for full text

name: getFlagSizeLimitsUsage
description: Returns whether angular size limits are applied to filter DSOs by size.

name: setFlagSizeLimitsUsage
description: Enables or disables angular size limits for filtering deep-sky objects.

parameters.name: enabled
parameters.description: true to enable size limits, false to disable

name: getMinSizeLimit
description: Returns the minimum angular size (in arcminutes) for DSOs to be displayed when size limits are enabled.
example:
var minSize = NebulaMgr.getMinSizeLimit();
core.debug("Minimum DSO size: " + minSize + " arcmin");

name: setMinSizeLimit
description: Sets the minimum angular size (in arcminutes) for DSOs to be displayed. Objects smaller than this are filtered out.
example:
// Only show DSOs larger than 10 arcminutes
NebulaMgr.setMinSizeLimit(10.0);

name: getMaxSizeLimit
description: Returns the maximum angular size (in arcminutes) for DSOs to be displayed when size limits are enabled.
example:
var maxSize = NebulaMgr.getMaxSizeLimit();
core.debug("Maximum DSO size: " + maxSize + " arcmin");

name: setMaxSizeLimit
description: Sets the maximum angular size (in arcminutes) for DSOs to be displayed. Objects larger than this are filtered out.
example:
// Only show DSOs smaller than 60 arcminutes (1 degree)
NebulaMgr.setMaxSizeLimit(60.0);

name: getFlagShowOnlyNamedDSO
description: Returns whether only deep-sky objects with common names are displayed. Objects without common names are hidden.

name: setFlagShowOnlyNamedDSO
description: Sets whether only deep-sky objects with common names should be displayed. Useful for reducing clutter.

parameters.name: enabled
parameters.description: true to show only named DSOs, false to show all

name: getFlagUseTypeFilters
description: Returns whether type filters are currently enabled. When disabled, all object types are shown regardless of filter settings.

name: setFlagUseTypeFilters
description: Enables or disables type filters. When enabled, only object types selected in typeFilters will be displayed.

parameters.name: enabled
parameters.description: true to enable type filters, false to disable

name: getCirclesColor
description: Returns the default color used for nebula symbols (circles, etc.) for objects without specific type coloring.

name: setCirclesColor
description: Sets the default color used for nebula symbols (circles, etc.) for objects without specific type coloring.

name: getRegionsColor
description: Returns the color used for region symbols (dashed shapes representing sky regions).

name: setRegionsColor
description: Sets the color used for region symbols (dashed shapes representing sky regions).

name: getGalaxyColor
description: Returns the color used for galaxy symbols (ellipses).

name: setGalaxyColor
description: Sets the color used for galaxy symbols (ellipses).

name: getActiveGalaxyColor
description: Returns the color used for active galaxy symbols.

name: setActiveGalaxyColor
description: Sets the color used for active galaxy symbols.

name: getRadioGalaxyColor
description: Returns the color used for radio galaxy symbols.

name: setRadioGalaxyColor
description: Sets the color used for radio galaxy symbols.

name: getInteractingGalaxyColor
description: Returns the color used for interacting galaxy symbols.

name: setInteractingGalaxyColor
description: Sets the color used for interacting galaxy symbols.

name: getQuasarColor
description: Returns the color used for quasar symbols.

name: setQuasarColor
description: Sets the color used for quasar symbols.

name: getPossibleQuasarColor
description: Returns the color used for possible quasar symbols.

name: setPossibleQuasarColor
description: Sets the color used for possible quasar symbols.

name: getClusterColor
description: Returns the color used for star cluster symbols (both open and globular).

name: setClusterColor
description: Sets the color used for star cluster symbols (both open and globular).

name: getOpenClusterColor
description: Returns the color used for open star cluster symbols.

name: setOpenClusterColor
description: Sets the color used for open star cluster symbols.

name: getGlobularClusterColor
description: Returns the color used for globular star cluster symbols (circled cross).

name: setGlobularClusterColor
description: Sets the color used for globular star cluster symbols (circled cross).

name: getStellarAssociationColor
description: Returns the color used for stellar association symbols.

name: setStellarAssociationColor
description: Sets the color used for stellar association symbols.

name: getStarCloudColor
description: Returns the color used for star cloud symbols.

name: setStarCloudColor
description: Sets the color used for star cloud symbols.

name: getNebulaColor
description: Returns the color used for bright nebula symbols (emission nebula boxes, etc.).

name: setNebulaColor
description: Sets the color used for bright nebula symbols (emission nebula boxes, etc.).

name: getPlanetaryNebulaColor
description: Returns the color used for planetary nebula symbols.

name: setPlanetaryNebulaColor
description: Sets the color used for planetary nebula symbols.

name: getReflectionNebulaColor
description: Returns the color used for reflection nebula symbols.

name: setReflectionNebulaColor
description: Sets the color used for reflection nebula symbols.

name: getBipolarNebulaColor
description: Returns the color used for bipolar nebula symbols.

name: setBipolarNebulaColor
description: Sets the color used for bipolar nebula symbols.

name: getEmissionNebulaColor
description: Returns the color used for emission nebula symbols.

name: setEmissionNebulaColor
description: Sets the color used for emission nebula symbols.

name: getDarkNebulaColor
description: Returns the color used for dark nebula symbols (gray boxes).

name: setDarkNebulaColor
description: Sets the color used for dark nebula symbols (gray boxes).
example:
// Set dark nebulae to dark gray
NebulaMgr.setDarkNebulaColor(core.vec3f(0.3, 0.3, 0.3));

name: getHydrogenRegionColor
description: Returns the color used for HII region (ionized hydrogen) symbols.

name: setHydrogenRegionColor
description: Sets the color used for HII region (ionized hydrogen) symbols.

name: getSupernovaRemnantColor
description: Returns the color used for supernova remnant symbols.

name: setSupernovaRemnantColor
description: Sets the color used for supernova remnant symbols.

name: getSupernovaCandidateColor
description: Returns the color used for supernova candidate symbols.

name: setSupernovaCandidateColor
description: Sets the color used for supernova candidate symbols.

name: getSupernovaRemnantCandidateColor
description: Returns the color used for supernova remnant candidate symbols.

name: setSupernovaRemnantCandidateColor
description: Sets the color used for supernova remnant candidate symbols.

name: getInterstellarMatterColor
description: Returns the color used for interstellar matter symbols.

name: setInterstellarMatterColor
description: Sets the color used for interstellar matter symbols.

name: getEmissionObjectColor
description: Returns the color used for emission object symbols.

name: setEmissionObjectColor
description: Sets the color used for emission object symbols.

name: getBlLacObjectColor
description: Returns the color used for BL Lac object symbols.

name: setBlLacObjectColor
description: Sets the color used for BL Lac object symbols.

name: getBlazarColor
description: Returns the color used for blazar symbols.

name: setBlazarColor
description: Sets the color used for blazar symbols.

name: getMolecularCloudColor
description: Returns the color used for molecular cloud symbols.

name: setMolecularCloudColor
description: Sets the color used for molecular cloud symbols.

name: getYoungStellarObjectColor
description: Returns the color used for young stellar object (YSO) symbols.

name: setYoungStellarObjectColor
description: Sets the color used for young stellar object (YSO) symbols.

name: getPossiblePlanetaryNebulaColor
description: Returns the color used for possible planetary nebula symbols.

name: setPossiblePlanetaryNebulaColor
description: Sets the color used for possible planetary nebula symbols.

name: getProtoplanetaryNebulaColor
description: Returns the color used for protoplanetary nebula symbols.

name: setProtoplanetaryNebulaColor
description: Sets the color used for protoplanetary nebula symbols.

name: getStarColor
description: Returns the color used for star symbols (within DSO catalogs).

name: setStarColor
description: Sets the color used for star symbols (within DSO catalogs).

name: getSymbioticStarColor
description: Returns the color used for symbiotic star symbols.

name: setSymbioticStarColor
description: Sets the color used for symbiotic star symbols.

name: getEmissionLineStarColor
description: Returns the color used for emission-line star symbols.

name: setEmissionLineStarColor
description: Sets the color used for emission-line star symbols.

name: getGalaxyClusterColor
description: Returns the color used for galaxy cluster symbols.

name: setGalaxyClusterColor
description: Sets the color used for galaxy cluster symbols.

name: getCatalogFilters
description: Returns the current catalog filter flags as an integer bitmask. Determines which astronomical catalogs are displayed (NGC, IC, Messier, etc.).
example:
var catalogFilters = NebulaMgr.getCatalogFilters();
core.debug("Catalog filters: " + catalogFilters);

name: setCatalogFilters
description: Sets the catalog filter flags. Controls which astronomical catalogs are displayed. Flags can be combined using bitwise OR (|).

parameters.name: flags
parameters.description: Catalog filter bitmask (CatNGC, CatIC, CatM, CatC, CatB, CatSh2, CatVdB, CatRCW, CatLDN, CatLBN, CatCr, CatMel, CatPGC, CatUGC, CatCed, CatArp, CatVV, CatPK, CatPNG, CatSNRG, CatACO, CatHCG, CatESO, CatVdBH, CatDWB, CatTr, CatSt, CatRu, CatVdBHa, CatOther, CatAll)

name: selectAllCatalogs
description: Enables display of all available deep-sky catalogs. Shows all objects from all catalog sources.

name: selectStandardCatalogs
description: Enables a standard selection of catalogs: Messier (M), NGC, and IC. This is a good default for most observers.
example:
// Show only M, NGC, IC catalogs
NebulaMgr.selectStandardCatalogs();

name: selectNoneCatalogs
description: Disables all catalog filters, hiding all deep-sky objects regardless of their catalog origin.

name: loadCatalogFilters
description: Loads catalog filter settings from the Stellarium configuration file (config.ini).
example:
// Reload catalog filters from config
NebulaMgr.loadCatalogFilters();

name: storeCatalogFilters
description: Saves current catalog filter settings to the Stellarium configuration file (config.ini).
example:
// Save current catalog filters to config
NebulaMgr.storeCatalogFilters();

name: getTypeFilters
description: Returns the current type filter flags as an integer bitmask. Determines which types of deep-sky objects are displayed (galaxies, clusters, nebulae, etc.).
example:
var typeFilters = NebulaMgr.getTypeFilters();
core.debug("Type filters: " + typeFilters);

name: setTypeFilters
description: Sets the type filter flags. Controls which types of deep-sky objects are displayed. Flags can be combined using bitwise OR (|).

parameters.name: flags
parameters.description: Type filter bitmask (TypeGalaxies, TypeActiveGalaxies, TypeInteractingGalaxies, TypeOpenStarClusters, TypeGlobularStarClusters, TypeHydrogenRegions, TypeBrightNebulae, TypeDarkNebulae, TypePlanetaryNebulae, TypeSupernovaRemnants, TypeGalaxyClusters, TypeOther, TypeAll)

name: searchM
description: Searches for a deep-sky object by its Messier catalog number (e.g., M31, M42, M45).
returnDescription: Nebula object pointer, or null if not found

name: searchNGC
description: Searches for a deep-sky object by its NGC (New General Catalogue) number.
returnDescription: Nebula object pointer, or null if not found

name: searchIC
description: Searches for a deep-sky object by its IC (Index Catalogue) number.
returnDescription: Nebula object pointer, or null if not found
example:
// Find IC 434 (Horsehead Nebula region)
var ic434 = NebulaMgr.searchIC(434);

name: searchForCommonName
description: Searches for a deep-sky object by its common name (e.g., 'Andromeda Galaxy', 'Orion Nebula', 'Pleiades').

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Common name of the deep-sky object (case-insensitive)

returnDescription: Nebula object pointer, or null if not found

name: getLatestSelectedDSODesignation
description: Returns the catalog designation (e.g., 'M31', 'NGC 224') of the most recently selected deep-sky object.
example:
var lastDSO = NebulaMgr.getLatestSelectedDSODesignation();
core.debug("Last selected DSO: " + lastDSO);

name: getLatestSelectedDSODesignationWIC
description: Returns the catalog designation of the most recently selected deep-sky object without considering catalog filter settings.
example:
var lastDSO = NebulaMgr.getLatestSelectedDSODesignationWIC();
core.debug("Last selected DSO (without filter): " + lastDSO);

name: getAllDeepSkyObjects
description: Returns a list containing all deep-sky objects (nebulae, galaxies, clusters) loaded in Stellarium.
example:
var allDSO = NebulaMgr.getAllDeepSkyObjects();
core.debug("Total DSO loaded: " + allDSO.length);

name: getDeepSkyObjectsByType
description: Returns a list of deep-sky objects filtered by type. Types can be numeric constants (e.g., '100' for Messier, '108' for NGC).

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Object type identifier (as string or integer)

name: computeMaxMagHint
description: Computes the maximum magnitude for which nebula hints will be displayed based on current sky conditions and hint amount settings.

parameters.name: skyDrawer
parameters.description: The sky drawer object containing current sky conditions
